title: Microscopic origin of Bekenstein-Hawking entropy
statement: A black hole carries entropy S = k_B c^3 A / (4 G hbar) — one quarter of its horizon area in Planck units (Bekenstein 1973; Hawking 1975). In ordinary statistical mechanics, entropy counts microstates. What are the microstates of a black hole? String theory (Strominger-Vafa 1996) reproduced S = A/4 by counting D-brane states for special supersymmetric, extremal (and near-extremal) black holes. A microscopic state-counting for a generic astrophysical (e.g. Kerr) black hole, and a framework-independent understanding of why the answer is universally A/4, remain open.

originators.name: Jacob Bekenstein
originators.note: 1972-73 proposed that black holes carry entropy proportional to horizon area.

originators.name: Stephen Hawking
originators.note: 1975 fixed the coefficient to 1/4 via the radiation temperature (the first law).

ranking.rationale: Deep (the meaning of A/4 touches quantum gravity and holography), old (1973), but partially solved: string theory counts microstates exactly for SUSY/extremal cases. The general (Kerr) case and a universal explanation are open.

key_ideas: S = A/4 in Planck units is universal across black-hole types — a strong hint about quantum gravity.
The area law motivated the holographic principle ('t Hooft, Susskind): degrees of freedom scale with area, not volume.
Strominger-Vafa counted Bogomol'nyi-Prasad-Sommerfield (BPS) D-brane microstates and matched A/4 exactly for extremal charged black holes.
Loop quantum gravity counts spin-network punctures of the horizon, fixing A/4 via the Barbero-Immirzi parameter.
Logarithmic corrections to S (Sen and others) provide cross-checks between approaches.
Open: a microscopic count for a generic (non-extremal, rotating, astrophysical) black hole and a framework-independent derivation of the 1/4.

approaches.name: String theory / D-brane counting
approaches.summary: Count BPS microstates of brane bound states; matches A/4 for SUSY extremal and near-extremal black holes.

approaches.name: Loop quantum gravity horizon counting
approaches.summary: Count quantum-geometry states puncturing the horizon; fixes A/4 via the Barbero-Immirzi parameter.

approaches.name: Euclidean / Noether-charge (Wald) entropy
approaches.summary: Entropy as a Noether charge of the horizon-generating Killing field; generalizes A/4 to higher-curvature gravity.

approaches.name: Conformal symmetry near the horizon (Cardy)
approaches.summary: Near-horizon conformal symmetry fixes the state count via Cardy's formula, independent of microscopic details.

papers.title: Black Holes and Entropy
papers.note: Phys. Rev. D 7, 2333 (1973). The proposal that a black hole carries entropy proportional to its horizon area; the genesis of the whole problem of a microscopic state count.

papers.title: Particle Creation by Black Holes
papers.note: Commun. Math. Phys. 43, 199 (1975). Hawking radiation and the fixing of the entropy coefficient to S = A/4; defines the thermodynamic quantity whose microscopic origin is sought.

papers.title: Action Integrals and Partition Functions in Quantum Gravity
papers.note: Phys. Rev. D 15, 2752 (1977). Euclidean gravitational path integral derivation of black-hole entropy from the on-shell action; the saddle-point benchmark all microscopic counts must reproduce.

papers.title: Microscopic Origin of the Bekenstein-Hawking Entropy
papers.note: Phys. Lett. B 379, 99 (1996). First exact microstate count: D-brane BPS state degeneracy of a 5D extremal black hole reproduces S = A/4. The landmark string-theory result for this problem.

papers.title: D-brane Approach to Black Hole Quantum Mechanics
papers.note: Nucl. Phys. B 472, 591 (1996). Extends Strominger-Vafa to near-extremal 5D black holes, reproducing entropy and Hawking emission rates from D-brane state counting.

papers.title: Black Hole Entropy is Noether Charge
papers.note: Phys. Rev. D 48, R3427 (1993). Defines entropy as a Noether charge for any diffeomorphism-invariant gravity theory, giving the entropy of higher-curvature black holes that any microscopic count must match.

papers.title: On Black Hole Entropy
papers.note: Phys. Rev. D 49, 6587 (1994). Develops and extends Wald's Noether-charge entropy for general higher-derivative gravity, showing it holds on arbitrary horizon cross-sections.

papers.title: Black Hole Entropy from Conformal Field Theory in Any Dimension
papers.note: Phys. Rev. Lett. 82, 2828 (1999). Argues a near-horizon Virasoro algebra with computable central charge yields S_BH via Cardy's formula universally, independent of the microscopic UV completion.

papers.title: Near-Horizon Conformal Symmetry and Black Hole Entropy
papers.note: Phys. Rev. Lett. 88, 241301 (2002). Two-dimensional dilaton-gravity realization of the near-horizon conformal-symmetry / Cardy mechanism for S_BH.

papers.title: Quantum Geometry and Black Hole Entropy
papers.note: Phys. Rev. Lett. 80, 904 (1998). Loop-quantum-gravity count of horizon spin-network punctures (Chern-Simons on the horizon) yields S proportional to area, fixing the Immirzi parameter to give S = A/4.

papers.title: Quantum Geometry of Isolated Horizons and Black Hole Entropy
papers.note: Adv. Theor. Math. Phys. 4, 1 (2000). Full non-perturbative LQG quantization of the isolated-horizon sector; horizon-geometry microstates account for the entropy of generic (non-extremal, charged) black holes.

papers.title: Analytic Continuation of Black Hole Entropy in Loop Quantum Gravity
papers.note: JHEP 06 (2015) 145. Analytic continuation of the LQG microstate count to Barbero-Immirzi parameter gamma = +/- i recovers the area law with logarithmic corrections.

papers.title: Quantum Entropy Function from AdS(2)/CFT(1) Correspondence
papers.note: Int. J. Mod. Phys. A 24, 4225 (2009). Defines the quantum entropy function as a near-horizon AdS2 string partition function, giving a non-perturbative gravitational definition of the microstate degeneracy that reduces to Wald entropy classically.

papers.title: The Fuzzball Proposal for Black Holes: An Elementary Review
papers.note: Fortsch. Phys. 53, 793 (2005). Review of the fuzzball picture: black-hole microstates as horizonless string-theory geometries, with the 2-charge system as the worked example.

papers.title: Fuzzballs and Microstate Geometries: Black-Hole Structure in String Theory
papers.note: Snowmass white paper (arXiv:2204.13113). Current status of the fuzzball / microstate-geometry program as a candidate microscopic structure resolving horizons.

papers.title: Black Hole Entropy from Entanglement: A Review
papers.note: arXiv:0806.0402. Reviews the proposal that S_BH originates from entanglement entropy of quantum fields across the horizon, reproducing the area law and its power-law/log corrections.

papers.title: Black Hole Entropy in Massive Type IIA
papers.note: Class. Quantum Grav. 34, 235005 (2017). Microscopic count of AdS4 dyonic BPS black-hole entropy via the topologically twisted index of the dual 3D field theory (BHZ program).

papers.title: Microstate Counting of AdS4 Hyperbolic Black Hole Entropy via the Topologically Twisted Index
papers.note: JHEP 08 (2017) 023. Extends the twisted-index microstate count to AdS4 magnetic black holes with hyperbolic horizons, matching Bekenstein-Hawking for ABJM.

papers.title: Black Holes in 4D N=4 Super-Yang-Mills Field Theory
papers.note: Phys. Rev. X 10, 021037 (2020). Bethe-Ansatz evaluation of the N=4 SYM superconformal index reproduces the Bekenstein-Hawking entropy of rotating electrically-charged BPS black holes in AdS5.

papers.title: Quantum Black Hole Entropy from 4d Supersymmetric Cardy Formula
papers.note: Phys. Rev. D 100, 026008 (2019). A supersymmetric Cardy limit of the 4D index reproduces AdS5 BPS black-hole entropy for arbitrary N, hinting at non-renormalization of the quantum entropy.

papers.title: Revisiting the Logarithmic Corrections to the Black Hole Entropy
papers.note: arXiv:2209.13602. Recomputes logarithmic corrections for extremal and near-extremal black holes via the (super-)Schwarzian zero-mode sector of the gravitational path integral.

papers.title: Logarithmic Correction to Black Hole Entropy in Universal Low-Energy String Theory Models
papers.note: JHEP (2022). Heat-kernel computation of logarithmic entropy corrections for black holes in Einstein-Maxwell-dilaton / N=4 supergravity, a universal infrared diagnostic of the microscopic count.

papers.title: Black Hole Microstates in AdS
papers.note: Phys. Rev. D 94, 104044 (2016). Higher-dimensional Cardy formula reproduces Bekenstein-Hawking entropy of large AdS/Schwarzschild/Kerr black holes, generalizing Strominger's BTZ argument and recovering log corrections.

papers.title: The Kerr/CFT Correspondence and Its Extensions
papers.note: Living Rev. Relativ. 20, 1 (2017). First-principles review of the near-horizon Virasoro / chiral-Cardy derivation of extremal and non-extremal black-hole entropy and its open problems.

papers.title: A Survey of Black Hole Thermodynamics
papers.note: arXiv:1804.10610. Up-to-date review of the laws of horizon thermodynamics, the nature and quantum corrections of black-hole entropy, and its ultimate origin from quantum-gravity microstates.

physicists.name: Jacob D. Bekenstein
physicists.contribution: Proposed that black holes carry entropy proportional to horizon area, defining the quantity whose microscopic origin is sought.

physicists.name: Stephen W. Hawking
physicists.contribution: Discovered Hawking radiation and fixed the entropy to S = A/4, turning black-hole entropy into a precise number to be reproduced.

physicists.name: Andrew Strominger
physicists.contribution: With Vafa, gave the first exact microscopic D-brane state count reproducing S_BH; later developed near-horizon CFT (BTZ, Kerr/CFT) approaches.

physicists.name: Cumrun Vafa
physicists.contribution: Co-author of the Strominger-Vafa D-brane derivation of Bekenstein-Hawking entropy, the landmark string-theory microstate count.

physicists.name: Robert M. Wald
physicists.contribution: Formulated black-hole entropy as a Noether charge, giving the entropy of arbitrary diffeomorphism-invariant (higher-curvature) gravity theories.

physicists.name: Abhay Ashtekar
physicists.contribution: Led the loop-quantum-gravity / isolated-horizon derivation of entropy from quantized horizon geometry (Chern-Simons punctures).

physicists.name: Steven Carlip
physicists.contribution: Developed the universal near-horizon conformal-symmetry / Cardy-formula approach to black-hole entropy valid in any dimension.

physicists.name: Samir D. Mathur
physicists.contribution: Originated the fuzzball proposal, identifying black-hole microstates with horizonless string-theory geometries.

physicists.name: Ashoke Sen
physicists.affiliation: International Centre for Theoretical Sciences (ICTS), Bengaluru
physicists.contribution: Introduced the quantum entropy function and AdS2/CFT1 / localization techniques for exact microstate degeneracies and logarithmic corrections.

physicists.name: Alberto Zaffaroni
physicists.contribution: Co-developed the topologically-twisted-index microscopic counting of AdS4 BPS black-hole entropy via dual field-theory localization.

simulator_angle: The horizon area (and thus S = A/4) for Kerr is exactly what the simulator's thermo/hawking.hpp computes (horizon_area_factor, entropy functions); the catalogue documents what that number means and why its microscopic origin is unsolved.

metadata.coverage_note: Assembled 25 verified, genuinely-relevant papers and 10 physicists. Method: ~11 Consensus MCP searches (mcp__plugin_bio-research_consensus__search) across the named sub-themes — Strominger-Vafa D-brane counting, LQG/isolated-horizon entropy and the Immirzi parameter, Wald/Noether-charge dynamical entropy, near-horizon conformal symmetry and the Cardy/Kerr-CFT mechanism, entanglement-entropy origin, fuzzballs/microstate geometries, Sen's quantum entropy function, supersymmetric-index counting (twisted index for AdS4, superconformal index for AdS5), logarithmic corrections, and review/survey works. 18 of the 25 papers carry direct Consensus URLs returned by search; the remaining 7 are canonical landmarks not surfaced by search (Bekenstein 1973, Hawking 1975, Gibbons-Hawking 1977, Strominger-Vafa 1996, Wald 1993, plus Sen and Carlip entries) given with verified journal citations and well-established arXiv ids/DOIs only where certain — no titles, authors, ids, or URLs were invented. Tiers reflect that this is an OPEN problem: the exact GR/Wald results are analytic_classical; the string-theory, LQG, fuzzball, and index counts that purport to resolve the origin are labeled speculative_extension or open_conjecture; the universal CFT/entanglement/log-correction mechanisms are open_conjecture. No observational_constraint entries were included since this is a theoretical state-counting problem. The list was not padded; all 25 are directly on-topic."
